name: run_asset_import_script
description_lines: Run one bounded editor-side GDScript worker against an existing Godot-imported source asset and its `.import` configuration.

Use this tool for any source file that Godot has imported and that has a matching `.import` sidecar, including 3D assets, textures, audio, fonts, and importer-specific formats. Do not assume the imported resource type from the file extension alone.

Tool boundary: use this tool to inspect the generated resource or change how Godot imports the source everywhere. Use `run_scene_edit_script` to change nodes, transforms, materials, or other per-instance behavior in an authored `.tscn` or `.scn` scene.

Recommended workflow: start with `mode=inspect`; read `ctx.get_import_info()`; list only relevant options with `ctx.list_import_options(prefix)`; load the generated resource; branch on the reported importer or resource class; and log bounded, task-relevant summaries. Switch to `mode=edit` only after the exact option, current native type, and `editable=true` status have been observed.
The script contract is `@tool extends RefCounted` with `func run(ctx) -> void`.

Context edit helpers: `ctx.set_import_option(name, value)`, `ctx.get_staged_changes()`, and `ctx.discard_import_option_change(name)`. Import values use their native Godot Variant types. Preserve the type returned by `get_import_option()`.

`ctx.get_dependencies()` reports upstream files needed to import this asset. It does not find downstream project consumers such as scenes using a model, materials using a texture, scripts playing audio, or themes using a font.
In inspect mode, setters fail and no import settings are saved. In edit mode, changes are staged until the worker exits successfully.

Fennara owns `.import` persistence, calls the live editor's `EditorFileSystem.reimport_files()`, verifies canonical settings, generated outputs, filesystem validity, and a deep resource reload, then attempts to restore and reimport the previous configuration if requested verification fails. Recovery success or failure is reported explicitly.
Do not write `.import` files directly. Direct sidecar modification is detected, restored, and reported as failure.

Version one edits only safe existing cache-generating options for built-in texture and scene importers. `_subresources`, importer identity, import scripts, external extraction paths, and unknown importer effects are inspect-only.

`ctx.get_imported_resource()` is loaded with `CACHE_MODE_IGNORE_DEEP`. `ctx.instantiate_imported_scene()` places an owned temporary PackedScene instance in the live editor SceneTree and removes it after the worker. It refuses imported scenes with `nodes/root_script` configured.
Provide exactly one of `code` or `script_path`. On retries, patch the returned `script_path` and reuse it.

Worker syntax is checked through Fennara's shared GDScript language-server diagnostics before execution. If the language server is unavailable, Godot's native script loader remains the final safety check and rejects invalid workers.

Keep logs bounded. Summarize meshes, materials, animations, dependencies, and other large data instead of logging raw arrays, vertices, animation keys, texture bytes, or the full `_subresources` dictionary.

asset_path.description: Existing imported source path under `res://`. The matching `<asset_path>.import` sidecar must already exist.

mode.description: Use `inspect` first for read-only imported-resource and option inspection. Use `edit` only after inspection has identified an exact supported option and its native type; successful edits automatically reimport and verify through the live editor.

code.description: Raw `@tool extends RefCounted` GDScript defining `func run(ctx) -> void`. Keep it targeted to one asset and emit bounded summaries with `ctx.log()`. Provide exactly one of `code` or `script_path`.

script_path.description: Existing `.gd` worker script. Prefer patching and reusing this path after a failed attempt. Provide exactly one of `code` or `script_path`.
